methodsAttempted.query: site:hse.ru/en artificial intelligence ChatGPT students policy HSE University
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the official HSE Academic Handbook page titled HSE University's Policy on Artificial Intelligence Use in Student Works.

methodsAttempted.query: site:hse.ru ИИ ChatGPT генеративный искусственный интеллект студенты преподаватели правила ВШЭ
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Russian official HSE news and publication pages; the English policy page linked to the Russian canonical handbook page.
The Russian AI-guidelines page linked official documents on plagiarism/generative-model checking, ethical principles for AI systems, interim assessment rules, and student internal regulations.
robots.txt allows the handbook and Russian docs pages used here, includes crawl-delay 3, and disallows /en/docs/ plus /data/xf/ raw document paths.

artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery found a central HSE Academic Handbook policy page on student AI use in Russian and English, plus an official Russian legal-documents page for plagiarism and generative-model checking. The AI ethics declaration page timed out during Firecrawl extraction, and an HSE news item was treated as research/news rather than policy evidence.

HSE University's Policy on Artificial Intelligence Use in Student Works
Регламент организации проверки письменных учебных работ на наличие плагиата, использования генеративных моделей и размещения выпускных квалификационных работ

artifacts.claimText: HSE University states that there is no university-wide prohibition on student use of AI, and students may use generative models in academic work when they follow disclosure requirements.

citation.citationTitle: Правила использования искусственного интеллекта студентами НИУ ВШЭ

artifacts.claimText: HSE University requires students to disclose use of generative AI in written academic work, including the parts prepared with AI, the purpose and method of use, model name or source, and an effectiveness evaluation.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow AI-tool-treatment claim because the Russian official handbook page explicitly says there is no university-wide ban and allows generative-model use in academic work.
Approved because the policy uses mandatory Russian language for student disclosure and lists required declaration contents.
Approved with may-prohibit wording, matching the source language and avoiding a university-wide prohibition claim.
Approved as source_status only; the page metadata supports existence and university scope but the full DOCX text was not staged.

artifacts.title: HSE University AI Policy Staged Evidence
artifacts.summary: HSE University has an official Academic Handbook page in Russian and English stating that student AI use is not banned university-wide, but must be disclosed; instructors may prohibit generative-model use for assessment elements when stated in the syllabus. A linked official regulation page covers checking written student work for plagiarism and generative-model use.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The official AI ethics declaration page was discovered but not used as evidence because Firecrawl extraction timed out in this run.
Official news and research pages about ChatGPT were rejected as non-policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
